name: Pilaf with Sour Cherries and Lentils
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-5 liter Dutch oven, melt most of the butter and slowly brown the onions. Add the cleaned lentils and fry a bit; then add the cleaned rice. Stir constantly, browning the rice without letting it stick.
2. Add the cherries and 550 ml liquid made up of cherry juice, stock, and water. Add 1-5 ml turmeric and 1 ml ground cumin if desired; adjust salt (depending on broth's saltiness).
3. Bring to a boil, stir with a fork, cover tightly, and let cook over the very lowest heat for about 20 minutes.
4. Fluff up the rice with a fork (never a spoon) and add the remaining butter to the bottom of the pot.
5. Raise the heat slightly for 5-10 minutes to form a crust on the bottom (possible without this step).
6. Serve, ensuring each serving includes some crust.
Author's Notes:
This Pilaf with sour cherries and lentils is a Persian-style dish, though not authentically verified. It can be served as dinner or paired with spiced grilled chicken or lamb stew. Important ingredient notes are provided.
An excellent side dish is strained yoghurt (Lebany Spread) or fresh yoghurt, possibly flavored with herbs like parsley, coriander, mint; spices such as paprika, black pepper, onion seed, olive oil, and lemon juice. Basmati or Patna rice from India/Pakistan offers a flavorful long-grain option worth the premium price.
Red lentils are half the size of brown lentils; avoid substituting with larger ones which may not cook properly. Rinse thoroughly to remove dust and pebbles. Sour cherries, available fresh for short periods and canned in stores near fruit or baking supplies, should be used sparingly sweet preserves are unsuitable.
Almost any stock or broth works, including chicken or lamb; canned options require careful salt monitoring.
